risk_if_not_implemented: Without Certificate Denylisting, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Network Intrusion Detection / Prevention Systems (NIDS / NIPS)
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to employ Network Intrusion Detection / Prevention Systems (NIDS/NIPS) to detect and/or prevent intrusions into the network.
compensating_control_1.justification: Network Intrusion Detection / Prevention Systems (NIDS / NIPS) (NET-08) provides detective monitoring capability that compensates for the absence of Certificate Denylisting (NET-18.9) by identifying unauthorized, anomalous, or non-compliant activity that the primary control would have prevented. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: DNS & Content Filtering
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to force Internet-bound network traffic through a proxy device (e.g., Policy Enforcement Point (PEP)) for URL content filtering and DNS filtering to limit a user's ability to connect to dangerous or prohibited Internet sites.
compensating_control_2.justification: DNS & Content Filtering (NET-18) provides network-level access restriction that compensates for the absence of Certificate Denylisting (NET-18.9) by limiting attacker reach and lateral movement opportunities across the environment. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
